blocks.content: The old water tower sat at the edge of a field past the last residential street, fenced off with the same green mesh the town used for everything it wanted to look like it had handled.
They locked their bikes to the fence. Daisy locked hers and Savannah locked hers. Kit leaned his against the fence.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: One day someone's going to steal that bike.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: Nobody's going to steal that bike.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: It is. I have excellent taste in bikes. Which is why nobody's going to steal it — they'd recognize the quality and feel bad.
Daisy made the face. The ceiling fan face.
They were crawling through the gap in the fence when they heard the voices.
Kit stood up on the other side. Three kids — Danny Pruett and two of his usual crew, coming around from the far side of the tower. They'd already been in.

blocks.speaker: Danny
blocks.content: Jenkins. Didn't know this was your property.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: It's not my property. It's not your property either.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: There's no 'first' to a public water tower, Danny.
Danny shifted his attention to Daisy.
Exact same pleasant tone she used for everyone. Somehow that made it worse for Danny, not better.
